mnemonics: Vote with Your Wallet (Weighted Votes): Imagine your 'k' neighbors are voting for a class label, but the ones closer to the point have more money (weight) to contribute to the vote, so their vote counts more.
keyPoints: Assigns greater influence (weight) to nearer neighbors in the classification/regression decision, often using the inverse of the distance (1/distance).
Mitigates the impact of outliers or noisy data points that might be included in the 'k' neighbors by giving them less weight.
Generally provides smoother and more accurate predictions than standard K-NN, especially when the data has varying densities.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to scale the features before calculating distances, which causes features with larger scales to disproportionately dominate the weighting.
Using a simple majority vote instead of a weighted vote when implementing the algorithm, thus nullifying the 'weighted' aspect.
Improperly handling zero distances, which would result in a division by zero error when using the common 1/distance weight formula.
